why: Tier3 TV feasible=False; n_eff_q25_tv=112 vs required≈252 (tv_window≈84)
Tier3 STATIC feasible=True; n_eff_q25_static=110 vs required≈98
Non-stationarity is not a blocker if TIME_VARYING_gVAR is feasible; it mainly affects STATIC feasibility.
overall.technical_summary: Tier3_LaggedDynamicNetwork chosen with STATIC_gVAR due to stationarity requirement. Overall readiness 94.6/100 (FullyReady). 7 ready vars out of 14 candidates; 0 hard drops, 7 soft drops due to non-stationarity signals. STATIC gVAR n_eff_q25=110 (≥≈98 required); TV option not feasible (n_eff_q25_tv=112, required≈252; window≈84). Time regularity is good (regular_or_quasi_regular, no irregulars/duplicates). Mean missingness ~21.3%. Non-stationarity signals drive exclusion of several vars from STATIC; nonstationarity is a blocker for STATIC but acceptable for TIME_VARYING gVAR if data suffices for smoothing/windowing. Overall, current evidence supports consolidating with STATIC_gVAR tier while maintaining data collection to improve TV viability in the future.
overall.client_friendly_summary: Your data looks ready for a solid dynamic network model, but some variables show non-stationary behavior. For now, we’ll use a stable,STATIC lagged model with 7 ready indicators. Time-varying analysis isn’t yet feasible with current data. To keep improving, collect more data and keep timing regular to reduce missing values and unlock the option of a fully time-varying model later.
next_steps: Increase data completeness to reduce missingness.
Focus on the 7 ready variables to stabilize the model; consider reevaluating the dropped ones after preprocessing.
Maintain strict time regularity to avoid irregular samples.
If possible, collect more observations to approach the time-varying gVAR feasibility threshold (252 points).
Assess and address non-stationarity in dropped variables (e.g., detrending, normalization) to potentially reintroduce them.
Re-run readiness checks after data updates to monitor changes in n_eff and missingness.
caveats: Non-stationarity concerns limit STATIC_gVAR readiness for several variables.
TIME_VARYING_gVAR remains infeasible at current sample size/window requirements.
Mean missingness around 21% can bias static estimates if not addressed.
Only 7 of 14 candidate variables are currently ready; generalization should be cautious.
Even with low collinearity observed, small sample size relative to variables can threaten stability.
Data smoothing/window choices will impact time-varying model feasibility once enough data exist.
Soft-excluded variables may hold domain-specific information; revisit if preprocessing changes.
Dependence on stationarity assumptions means future model updates should include thorough diagnostics.

nonstationarity_handling.statement: Non-stationarity is not a problem if TIME_VARYING_gVAR is feasible; it mainly limits STATIC_gVAR feasibility.

Stationarity_fraction in evidence: 1.0; stationarity_required is true.
